title: SELECT DISTINCT not removing duplicates
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, value FLOAT);
SELECT DISTINCT name, value FROM test_table;
SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN value > 2.0 THEN name ELSE NULL END as filtered_name FROM test_table;
SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
key_bug_features: SELECT DISTINCT not functioning as expected with duplicates
